claim: Claude Cowork is Anthropic's local AI agent platform that can autonomously interact with files, apps, and tools on your computer, designed for non-technical users
actionable: Use Claude Cowork to automate workflows on your local machine instead of relying on web-based chatbots for file manipulation and automation tasks

claim: Global instructions in Cowork apply to all sessions and can include personal context, working preferences, and constraints that should never be violated
actionable: Set up global instructions in Cowork settings with personality traits, communication style, and explicit guardrails before starting projects

claim: Skills in Cowork are reusable sets of instructions that can be applied across multiple tasks and shared with other users
actionable: Create and save skills for repetitive instructions like 'apply brand to visual things' so you don't have to re-prompt the same guidance each time

claim: Connectors link Cowork to third-party software (Gmail, Google Drive, Google Calendar, Microsoft 365, etc.) enabling agent-to-service integration
actionable: Connect Cowork to your email and calendar systems to enable autonomous email triaging and schedule-based automation

claim: Projects in Cowork are persistent workspaces that wrap a folder with custom instructions, memory, files, and connections—enabling complex multi-session workflows
actionable: Use Projects for long-running work like investment tracking or content creation where the agent needs to remember context across sessions

claim: The Productivity plugin in Cowork automatically maintains memory (claude.md, memory.md) for agents without requiring manual memory file updates
actionable: Install the Productivity plugin on all Projects to let Cowork automatically track and update agent memory instead of maintaining markdown files yourself

claim: Combining Claude Cowork with Claude Code (the coding agent) allows building complex workflows and dashboards that Cowork alone cannot manage
actionable: Switch to Claude Code when your Cowork tasks require significant software development or complex scripting beyond agent capabilities

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is Anthropic's open standard that allows agents to discover and use capabilities from third-party systems
actionable: Check if MCP servers exist for tools you want to integrate before building custom connectors
